pages.full_text:
off for the Channel. There has been a foiled terrorist attack on planes between US + England (29 Pakistan).
	The drive to the Channel was awful, + we didn't have much time to spare – rain, spray + a headwind. The man who asked whether we had turned off the gas didn't bother to check, so that we could hurry through to get on the train (the numbering system for calling us was unclear) but then we just sat infront of the barrier + missed the train.
	After that, the journey improved. We got to Di Simmonds in Oldham at 3.30. There we showed her [strikethrough:gods] grandson, little Harry, the camping van. Then we took Margaret + Helen back to Trumpley + then home to 86.
pages.notes: This is a continuation from a previous page; no handwritten page number visible at top-left. The word 'gods' appears struck through before 'grandson'. 'Trumpley' could also read 'Trompley' — best reading is Trumpley. '86' likely refers to a house address. The right margin shows gutter-overlap text belonging to the right page.

pages.full_text:
__Saturday October 7th__
	Simon, Eve + Tonic left home at [?11] for a 24 hour trip. The last 2 months has been full of weddings + getting Thomas into his flat, Peter off to China, John off to Warwick + Charles back to Malvern. It's a beautiful day We drove to Hanbury church – lovely red stone + arch over doors, only one high box pew remaining. A monastery was on the site in 800. Amazing view from hill (an Iron Age fort?) of Bredon, Malvern + Cotswolds.
	The nearby campsite was too boggy so we drove on, [?Rom Salt Road from] [?Pershore] to the Cottage of Content pub ** (loos but no shower). We walked along raised levees to Bideford on Avon. Lots of fruit bushes, mini plums, which Eve picked while Simon washed up. The local farms sold us fruit + veg. Free range cats.
	Bideford has an old Mediaeval Bridge, + cars parked to watch children playing foot ball. Super warm weather.
	We drank at our campsite pub. The publican + his girlfriend (?) were watching
pages.route_sketch_description: A small hand-drawn squiggly line appearing after 'Free range cats' — appears to be a simple route or road sketch with zigzag/wavy marks, no labelled places.
pages.notes: Year cannot be determined from this page alone; volume B covers 2003–2019. 'Saturday October 7th' as a Saturday falls on e.g. 2006, 2017 within the volume range — cannot confirm without further context. 'Rom Salt Road' is uncertain; could be 'Roman Salt Road'. 'Pershore' is a plausible reading for the partly legible word after 'from'. The text is cut off at the bottom ('were watching'). 'Tonic' is likely a dog's name. The squiggly line after 'Free range cats' may be a doodle or very rough route sketch.
